1: theme Praise the LORD! Praise the LORD from the heavens; praise him in the heights! Praise from the heavens and heights — Revelation 5:11-13 and Psalms 19:1-6 where the heavenly hosts and all creation erupt in praise. The chain of praise from heights to depths that Ps 148 initiates finds its focal point in the Lamb: to him who sits on the throne and to the Lamb be blessing and honor and glory and might forever and ever! (Revelation 5:13).
2: shadow Praise him, all his angels; praise him, all his hosts! Angels and heavenly hosts praising — Revelation 5:11-12: ten thousand times ten thousand and thousands of thousands, saying with a loud voice, Worthy is the Lamb who was slain, to receive power and wealth and wisdom and might and honor and glory and blessing! The angels who praised at Christ's birth (Luke 2:13-14) now praise the Lamb in glory — the same hosts, the same object of praise.
3: shadow Praise him, sun and moon, praise him, all you shining stars! Sun, moon, and stars praising — the darkened sun at Christ's crucifixion (Matthew 27:45: there was darkness over all the land) is the creation mourning; at his return creation is renewed and all things praise. Revelation 21:23: in the new creation the city has no need of sun or moon to shine on it, for the glory of God gives it light, and its lamp is the Lamb.
4: shadow Praise him, you highest heavens, and you waters above the heavens! The highest heavens praising — Hebrews 4:14: we have a great high priest who has passed through the heavens, Jesus, the Son of God. Christ's ascension takes him through the highest heavens; from there he pours out the Spirit (Acts 2:33) and intercedes (Hebrews 7:25). The heavens praise the one who passed through them and is now exalted above them all.
5: direct Let them praise the name of the LORD! For he commanded and they were created. He commanded and they were created — John 1:3: All things were made through him, and without him was not any thing made that was made. Colossians 1:16: by him all things were created, in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible. The command through which all things were created is the Word — who became flesh in Jesus Christ. Creation praises the name of the one who made it through the Son.
6: shadow And he established them forever and ever; he gave a decree, and it shall not pass away. Creation established forever — Hebrews 1:10-12 applies the psalm of creation to Christ: you, Lord, laid the foundation of the earth in the beginning... they will perish, but you remain... you are the same, and your years will have no end. The eternal decree that holds creation is the eternal Son who holds all things together (Colossians 1:17).
7: shadow Praise the LORD from the earth, you great sea creatures and all deeps, Praise from the earth's depths — Revelation 5:13: every creature in heaven and on earth and under the earth and in the sea, and all that is in them, saying, To him who sits on the throne and to the Lamb be blessing and honor and glory and might forever and ever! The sea creatures of Psalms 148:7 are included in the universal praise of Rev 5 directed to the Lamb.
8: shadow fire and hail, snow and mist, stormy wind fulfilling his word! Storm and fire fulfilling the word — Acts 2:2-3: at Pentecost a sound like a mighty rushing wind... and divided tongues as of fire appeared — the Spirit coming as wind and fire, fulfilling Christ's promise (John 16:7). The stormy wind that fulfills God's word is the Spirit of Christ sent out to complete what was promised.
9: shadow Mountains and all hills, fruit trees and all cedars! Mountains, hills, and trees praising — the eschatological vision of Romans 8:22: the whole creation has been groaning together in the pains of childbirth until now. Creation waits with eager longing (Romans 8:19) to join the praise of Ps 148 — the praise it was created for but cannot yet fully sing. At Christ's return this groaning becomes the universal hallelujah.
10: shadow Beasts and all livestock, creeping things and flying birds! All creatures praising — the comprehensive vision of creation's worship. Revelation 5:13 includes every creature in the praise of the Lamb. Christ's reign as the second Adam (1 Corinthians 15:45) restores the dominion over the creatures that the first Adam lost; under Christ's lordship, the creatures fulfill their created purpose of praise.
11: shadow Kings of the earth and all peoples, princes and all rulers of the earth! Kings and all peoples praising — the vision of Revelation 21:24-26: the kings of the earth will bring their glory into it (the New Jerusalem). Philippians 2:10-11: every knee should bow... and every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father. The universal lordship that Psalms 148:11 anticipates is fulfilled in the exaltation of Christ.
12: shadow Young men and maidens together, old men and children! All ages praising — the church that spans every generation: Acts 2:17: your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, and your young men shall see visions, and your old men shall dream dreams. Joel's promise poured out at Pentecost; the full generational spectrum of Psalms 148:12 finds its fulfillment in the Spirit-filled church gathered around Christ.
13: direct Let them praise the name of the LORD, for his name alone is exalted; his majesty is above earth and heaven. His name alone exalted above all — Philippians 2:9-11: God has highly exalted him and bestowed on him the name that is above every name, so that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, in heaven and on earth and under the earth. The name exalted above earth and heaven in Psalms 148:13 is the name of Jesus Christ, given by the Father to the crucified and risen Son.
14: shadow He has raised up a horn for his people, praise for all his saints, for the people of Israel who are near to him. Praise the LORD! Horn raised up for his people — Luke 1:68-69: Zechariah's prophecy: Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he has visited and redeemed his people and has raised up a horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David. The horn of Psalms 148:14 is the Christ-child born to Mary — the raised horn of the LORD's people who draws near all who were far off.
